articles.summary: 'The players will go out there on the pitch. And I'm certainly expecting a tough game.' -...
articles.context: Algeria and Switzerland have faced tensions in recent years, particularly following Algeria's controversial exit from the 2014 World Cup, where they were eliminated by Germany in the Round of 16. The match reignited discussions about player representation and identity, as many Algerian players are of French descent. Additionally, Algeria's ongoing struggles with political unrest since the Hirak protests in 2019 add a layer of complexity to their international sporting engagements.

articles.title: From Rongai's most wanted gangster to a responsible family man - Edwin Ombodo's story
articles.context: Edwin Ombodo's transformation from a notorious gangster in Rongai to a family man highlights the ongoing challenges of youth crime and rehabilitation in Kenya. The government's efforts to combat gang violence, particularly since the 2017 elections, have intensified, with initiatives like the National Youth Service aiming to reintegrate former gang members into society. Ombodo's story reflects the personal impact of these broader socio-political dynamics and the potential for change amidst ongoing struggles with crime and poverty.

articles.title: Ruto Cabinet dilemma: Who'll make way for gender balance?
articles.summary: The court has ordered Ruto to reconstitute his Cabinet within 120 days to comply with the...
articles.context: In 2022, President William Ruto's administration faced criticism for its failure to achieve the constitutional requirement of gender parity in government appointments. Following a petition by the Kenya Women Parliamentary Association, the High Court ruled in October 2023 that Ruto must reconstitute his Cabinet within 120 days to ensure compliance with the two-thirds gender rule established in the 2010 Constitution. This ruling intensifies the ongoing debate over women's representation in Kenyan politics.

articles.title: Snails, grasshoppers, and rabbits: My controversial menu
articles.summary: From rabbit farms in Nairobi to snail curries and Ugandan grasshoppers, I have eaten it all
articles.context: As Kenya grapples with food security challenges exacerbated by climate change and rising costs, alternative protein sources like snails and grasshoppers are gaining attention. The 2021 National Food and Nutrition Security Policy emphasizes diversifying diets to combat malnutrition, while the push for sustainable farming practices aligns with the African Union's Agenda 2063 goals. This context makes exploring unconventional foods not only a culinary adventure but also a reflection of broader socio-economic trends in the region.

articles.title: 10 counties where suppliers have waited the longest for pay
articles.summary: Mombasa, Nairobi, Wajir, Garissa and Machakos are among the worst counties to do business with.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has faced significant fiscal challenges, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and a rising public debt crisis, leading to delayed payments to suppliers. Counties like Mombasa and Nairobi, which are critical economic hubs, have struggled to manage their budgets effectively, resulting in strained relationships with local businesses. The ongoing political tensions surrounding the 2022 elections and subsequent governance issues have further complicated financial accountability and transparency in these regions.

articles.title: The CPC on its 105th anniversary: Longevity by design, not chanceBy our staff reporter
articles.summary: In politics, survival over a century is rarely accidental. More often, it signals an ability to adapt, consolidate, and deliver. The Communist Party of China (CPC), now 105 years old, stands as one of the clearest examples of this principle in practice. At a time when many political parties struggle to remain relevant beyond a […]
articles.context: The CPC's 105th anniversary comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing political turmoil, marked by the Tigray conflict and the recent peace agreement signed in November 2022. As Ethiopia navigates its complex relationship with China, the CPC's longevity offers a contrasting model of political stability. The Ethiopian government's engagement with Chinese investments and infrastructure projects highlights the significance of the CPC's strategies in shaping regional dynamics and influencing Ethiopia's political landscape.

articles.title: بعد تحرك ترامب.. مصر تحسم موقفها من العودة إلى مفاوضات سد النهضة
articles.summary: حددت مصر موقفها من استئناف مفاوضات سد النهضة الإثيوبي وذلك في أعقاب تصريحات الرئيس الأمريكي دونالد ترامب بشأن حرص واشنطن على المساعدة في التوصل إلى حل للأزمة. وكشف وزير الموار المائية والري المصري هاني سويلم أن القاهرة أوقفت رسميا مفاوضات سد النهضة منذ ديسمبر 2023، متهما إثيوبيا بمواصلة المراوغة والتنصل من التفاهمات وعدم الالتزام بقواعد …
articles.context: تعود أزمة سد النهضة إلى عام 2011 عندما بدأت إثيوبيا بناء السد على النيل الأزرق، مما أثار قلق مصر والسودان بشأن تأثيره على حصصهما من المياه. في عام 2015، تم توقيع اتفاق إعلان المبادئ بين الدول الثلاث، لكن المفاوضات تعثرت منذ ديسمبر 2023 بسبب اتهامات مصر لإثيوبيا بعدم الالتزام بالاتفاقات. تصريحات ترامب الأخيرة أضافت بُعدًا جديدًا، حيث تسعى الولايات المتحدة للوساطة في الأزمة.

articles.title: Awash Bank Prepares to Expand into Neighboring Countries; Awaits Central Bank Approval
articles.summary: Awash Bank, a renowned institution in Ethiopia, has officially announced its intention to expand beyond national borders into other regional markets. The bank has confirmed that it has submitted a formal request to the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) to realize this goal. Tsehay Shiferaw, CEO of Awash Bank, explained that the request is currently […]
articles.context: Awash Bank's expansion plans come amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms and the aftermath of the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has reshaped the banking landscape. The National Bank of Ethiopia's recent push for financial sector liberalization, highlighted by the 2021 Financial Sector Transformation Plan, aims to attract foreign investment and enhance regional integration. This move is significant as it reflects the bank's confidence in the stability and growth potential of neighboring markets like Sudan and Kenya.

articles.title: Kenyan court charges eight schoolgirls with murder over deadly dormitory fire that killed 16 girls
articles.summary: Students deny 16 murder counts over blaze that killed classmates at Utumishi Girls Academy
articles.context: The tragic dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls Academy on January 24, 2023, reflects ongoing concerns about school safety and governance in Kenya, particularly in the wake of the 2017 fire at Moi Girls High School that killed nine students. The case has sparked national debates about accountability in educational institutions and the treatment of minors in the justice system, especially following the 2021 enactment of the Justice for Victims of Sexual Offenses Act, which emphasizes victim rights.

articles.title: ASK Foundation, German doctors launch free cleft surgery for 20 children in Juba
articles.summary: ASK Foundation, in partnership with South Sudan’s Ministry of Health, Gudele General Hospital and German humanitarian medical organization Deutsche Cleft Kinderhilfe e.V., has launched a four-day free cleft lip and cleft palate surgery campaign aimed at transforming the lives of children born with the condition. The surgical outreach, which began on Wednesday at Gudele General […] The post ASK Foundation, German doctors launch free cleft surgery for 20 children in Juba appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing humanitarian crises since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by civil conflict and economic instability. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace but has struggled to improve healthcare access. With limited resources, initiatives like the ASK Foundation's cleft surgery campaign are crucial for addressing the healthcare needs of vulnerable populations, particularly in regions like Juba, where medical services are scarce.

articles.title: ملابس داخلية من الذهب.. من هي النائبة هند العباسي وقصة الأموال والذهب المضبوطين؟
articles.summary: تشهد الساحة السياسية في العراق تطورات متسارعة بعد حملة أمنية واسعة وصفت بأنها من أكبر حملات مكافحة الفساد في البلاد، أسفرت عن توقيف عدد من النواب والمسؤولين الحاليين والسابقين، بينهم النائبة هند محمد صالح حسن العباسي، في خطوة أعادت ملف الفساد إلى صدارة المشهد السياسي، وسط اهتمام شعبي وإعلامي واسع بملابسات القضية وحجم المضبوطات التي …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التطورات في العراق في ظل تصاعد الاحتجاجات الشعبية ضد الفساد المستشري منذ عام 2019، حيث طالب المتظاهرون بإصلاحات جذرية. كما أن الحملة الأمنية الحالية، التي بدأت في سبتمبر 2023، تستهدف شخصيات بارزة في الحكومة، مما يعكس التوترات السياسية بين القوى الشيعية والسنية. النائبة هند العباسي، التي تم توقيفها، تمثل جزءًا من الصراع على النفوذ داخل البرلمان العراقي.

articles.title: NCP replaces secretary general ahead of elections
articles.summary: South Sudan’s National Congress Party (NCP) has appointed Mohamed Almak as its new secretary general, The post NCP replaces secretary general ahead of elections appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The appointment of Mohamed Almak as the new secretary general of South Sudan's National Congress Party comes amid heightened political tensions as the country approaches its 2024 elections. The NCP, historically aligned with Sudan's ruling party, faces challenges in a fragmented political landscape, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil conflict. This leadership change may influence party dynamics and electoral strategies in a crucial transitional period for South Sudan.

articles.title: Legislature ratifies peace deal amendments, clears way for elections
articles.summary: South Sudan’s Transitional National Legislature on Wednesday ratified amendments to the Revitalized Peace Agreement, paving the way for national elections to proceed without first completing a permanent constitution and a national population and housing census. The amendments were approved during the Fourth Ordinary Sitting of the Transitional National Legislature, a joint session of the Transitional […] The post Legislature ratifies peace deal amendments, clears way for elections appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The ratification of amendments to the Revitalized Peace Agreement is significant as it marks a crucial step in South Sudan's ongoing transition from civil war to stability, following the 2018 peace deal brokered by the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD). With national elections anticipated in 2024, the decision to proceed without a permanent constitution or census reflects the urgency to solidify governance structures amid lingering tensions between President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar.

articles.title: Parliament rewrites peace deal to clear path for 2026 elections
articles.summary: South Sudan’s parliament on Wednesday approved controversial amendments to the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, removing The post Parliament rewrites peace deal to clear path for 2026 elections appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to end South Sudan's civil war, which erupted in 2013, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The recent amendments, approved on October 25, 2023, are seen as a strategic move by the ruling coalition, including President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar, to facilitate the upcoming 2026 elections amid ongoing tensions and disputes over power-sharing and governance structures.

articles.title: 49 children released from military training centers, says DDR commission
articles.summary: 49 children have been released from military training centers in Upper Nile, Ruweng Administrative Area, and Western Equatoria State, according to the South Sudan National Disarmament, Demobilization and Reintegration (DDR) Commission. The Commission Chairperson, General Ayuen Alier, said the releases took place between April and June this year following engagement missions to the affected areas. […] The post 49 children released from military training centers, says DDR commission appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The release of these 49 children comes amid ongoing efforts to implement the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aims to end years of civil war in South Sudan. The DDR Commission, established to facilitate the disarmament and reintegration of former combatants, faces challenges due to persistent violence and the recruitment of child soldiers by various armed groups. The situation in Upper Nile and Western Equatoria remains volatile, complicating peace efforts and humanitarian access.

articles.title: إحباط تهريب أسلحة وكمية من الذخائر بولاية نهر النيل
articles.summary: تمكنت إدارة مكافحة التهريب بولاية نهر النيل، بالتنسيق مع جهاز المخابرات العامة، من إحباط محاولة لتهريب كمية كبيرة من الأسلحة والذخائر، وذلك في عملية أمنية نوعية أسفرت عن ضبط عربة بوكس محملة بترسانة من الأسلحة والذخائر. وضمت المضبوطات مدفع دوشكا، وعدد (15) بندقية كلاشنكوف، و(8) مسدسات، و(80) خزنة فارغة، و(20) شريطاً لمدفع عيار (54)، بالإضافة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه العملية في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع منذ اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023. تتزايد المخاوف من انتشار الأسلحة غير المشروعة في ظل الفوضى الأمنية، مما يعزز من قدرة الجماعات المسلحة على تنفيذ عملياتها. كما أن ضبط هذه الأسلحة يعكس جهود الحكومة السودانية في مكافحة التهريب وتعزيز الأمن في مناطق النزاع.

articles.title: إبادة (5) أطنان من السلع منتهية الصلاحية بأسواق سنار
articles.summary: نفذت إدارة التجارة بوزارة المالية والتخطيط الاقتصادي بولاية سنار ممثلة في إدارة حماية المستهلك، اليوم عملية إبادة لنحو (5) أطنان من السلع والمواد منتهية الصلاحية، وذلك بحضور مقرر لجنة حماية المستهلك، ومباحث التمويل والأمن الاقتصادي وممثل صحة البيئة، وممثل إدارة الإنتاج الحيواني، إلى جانب عدد من الإدارات ذات الصلة. وأوضحت مدير إدارة حماية المستهلك بوزارة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه العملية في سياق جهود الحكومة السودانية لتعزيز الأمن الغذائي وحماية المستهلك في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية المستمرة منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2019. كما تعكس هذه الخطوة التزام وزارة المالية والتخطيط الاقتصادي بتطبيق القوانين المتعلقة بحماية المستهلك، وسط تحديات تتعلق بالفساد وسوء إدارة الموارد. يضاف إلى ذلك، أن ولاية سنار تعد من المناطق التي تعاني من نقص في الخدمات الأساسية، مما يجعل هذه الإجراءات أكثر أهمية.

articles.title: وزير الثقافة والإعلام : القوات المسلحة تتقدم في كافة المحاور بصلابة
articles.summary: أكد الأستاذ خالد الإعيسر وزير الثقافة والإعلام والآثار والسياحة أن القوات المسلحة والقوات المساندة لها تتقدم بصلابة في كافة محاور القتال. وأضاف الإعيسر أن القوات المسلحة تقاتل الآن في أقصي غرب البلاد وأن الإرادة الوطنية ماضية حتى تحرير أي شبر من البلاد. وأثنى الإعيسر على الدور الذي لعبه الإعلام الوطني في إسناد معركة الكرامة، والذي …
articles.context: تأتي تصريحات وزير الثقافة والإعلام خالد الإعيسر في وقت حرج للسودان، حيث تشهد البلاد تصاعدًا في النزاع المسلح بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع منذ أبريل 2023. هذا الصراع، الذي أدى إلى تدهور الأوضاع الإنسانية، يتطلب دعمًا إعلاميًا قويًا لتعزيز الروح الوطنية. كما أن التقدم العسكري في الغرب يعكس جهود الحكومة لاستعادة السيطرة على المناطق المتأثرة بالنزاع.

articles.title: تشيلسي يضم باليسترا مدافع أتالانتا حتى يونيو 2033
articles.summary: أعلن نادي تشيلسي بشكل رسمي مساء اليوم الأربعاء، عن ضم مدافع أتالانتا، ماركو باليسترا، خلال فترة الانتقالات الصيفية الجارية. أصدر النادي اللندني بيان كشف فيه عن توقيع باليسترا المدافع البالغ من العمر 21 عامًا عقدًا مع البلوز لمدة سبع سنوات مع النادي، يمتد حتى نهاية يونيو 2033. وتشير التقارير الإنجليزية، إلى أن تشيلسي سيدفع 55 …
articles.context: تأتي صفقة انتقال ماركو باليسترا إلى تشيلسي في وقت حساس لكرة القدم الأوروبية، حيث يسعى النادي اللندني لتعزيز صفوفه بعد موسم مخيب للآمال. كما أن هذه الصفقة تعكس التوجه المتزايد للأندية الكبرى نحو استقطاب المواهب الشابة، في ظل التحديات الاقتصادية التي تواجهها الأندية نتيجة تأثيرات جائحة كورونا. يُعتبر باليسترا أحد أبرز المدافعين الصاعدين في إيطاليا، مما يزيد من أهمية هذه الصفقة لتشيلسي.

articles.title: منظمة العفو تدعو إلى إرسال قوات للحماية في السودان بعد الكشف عن "تطهير عرقي" مع مخاوف من تكراره في الأُبيّض
articles.summary: اتهامات جديدة لقوات الدعم السريع بارتكاب جرائم ضد الإنسانية وتطهير عرقي في الفاشر، وتحذيرات أممية من تكرار الفظائع في مدينة الأبيض، وتصاعد الأزمة الإنسانية مع تطور القتال وتفشي الكوليرا، فضلاً عن هجوم على قاعدة أممية حدودية.
articles.context: تأتي هذه الدعوة من منظمة العفو في وقت حساس، حيث تصاعدت حدة النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع منذ اندلاع القتال في أبريل 2023. الفاشر، عاصمة ولاية شمال دارفور، شهدت انتهاكات جسيمة، بينما تتزايد المخاوف من تكرار هذه الفظائع في مدينة الأبيض، مما يعكس عدم الاستقرار المستمر في البلاد بعد سنوات من الصراع والنزاعات العرقية.

articles.title: Torit Mayor alarms over low marriage rates among youth
articles.summary: The Mayor of Torit, Eddy William, has expressed concern over what he described as a growing number of unmarried young people in Torit Municipality, after finding out that only a small number of in the area are married. He said that during a recent public meeting attended by about 2,000 young people, only three indicated […] The post Torit Mayor alarms over low marriage rates among youth appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The low marriage rates among youth in Torit reflect broader social challenges in South Sudan, where ongoing conflict and economic instability have disrupted traditional family structures. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to foster peace, but persistent violence and displacement have hindered community cohesion. As a result, young people face uncertainty about their futures, impacting their willingness to commit to marriage.

articles.title: Libya’s OLA Energy Finalizes Major Acquisition of TotalEnergies’ Ethiopian Assets
articles.summary: The Libyan OLA Energy Group has signed a full acquisition agreement for the Ethiopian assets of the French-owned TotalEnergies. This agreement, finalized in Paris, transfers ownership of nearly 120 fuel stations and a critical 13,000-cubic-meter fuel storage terminal. This move has enabled the Libyan government-backed company to become the largest foreign operator in Ethiopia’s retail […]
articles.context: The acquisition comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a two-year civil conflict that ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As the country seeks to stabilize and attract foreign investment, OLA Energy's entry strengthens Libya's economic ties in the Horn of Africa, while TotalEnergies had previously faced challenges in the region, including security concerns and operational disruptions. This deal marks a significant shift in Ethiopia's energy landscape and foreign investment dynamics.

articles.title: اليابان تبدأ تجارب سريرية على دواء قد يتيح إنماء أسنان جديدة
articles.summary: بدأ علماء يابانيون تجارب سريرية على دواء اجتاز بنجاح المرحلة الأولى من التجارب على متطوعين، قد يساعد البشر على إنماء أسنان جديدة لأول مرة. ويتوقع عرضه في الأسواق عام 2030. ووفقا لشركة Toregem BioPharma (TBP)، هذا الدواء مخصص بالدرجة الأولى للأشخاص الذين يعانون من فقدان الأسنان الخلقي، ولكن قد يفيد أيضا الأشخاص الذين فقدوا أسنانهم …
articles.context: تعتبر مشاكل صحة الفم والأسنان من القضايا الصحية المهمة في السودان، حيث يعاني العديد من السكان من نقص الرعاية الصحية الأساسية بسبب النزاعات المستمرة والأزمات الاقتصادية. في عام 2021، شهد السودان احتجاجات واسعة ضد الحكومة الانتقالية، مما أثر على الوصول إلى الخدمات الصحية. قد يوفر هذا الدواء الياباني الجديد حلاً مبتكرًا لمشكلة فقدان الأسنان، مما يعزز الصحة العامة في ظل الظروف الصعبة التي يواجهها المواطنون.

articles.title: بدء الترتيبات لزراعة القوقعة لطلاب مدرسة الصم والبكم بأم درمان
articles.summary: وقف مدير الإدارة العامة للطب العلاجي الدكتور محمد أحمد الشيخ اليوم على الترتيبات الخاصة ببدء برنامج عمليات زراعة القوقعة لطلاب وطالبات مدرسة الصم والبكم بأم درمان بدعم من والي ولاية الخرطوم. وجرى خلال زيارته لمدرسة الصم على عمليات حصر الحالات المستهدفة والاطمئنان على الجوانب الفنية والتنظيمية اللازمة لإنفاذ البرنامج، بما يضمن تقديم الخدمة للمستفيدين وفق …
articles.context: تأتي هذه المبادرة في سياق جهود الحكومة السودانية لتحسين الخدمات الصحية والتعليمية لذوي الاحتياجات الخاصة، خاصة في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والسياسية التي شهدها السودان منذ 2019. دعم والي ولاية الخرطوم، الذي تم تعيينه في 2021، يعكس التزام الحكومة بتحقيق التنمية المستدامة وتعزيز حقوق هذه الفئة. كما أن عمليات زراعة القوقعة تمثل خطوة مهمة في معالجة الفجوات في الرعاية الصحية للأطفال الصم والبكم.

articles.title: الميناء الجنوبي يستقبل 30 باخرة ويتداول أكثر من 26 ألف حاوية مكافئة في يونيو

articles.context: تأتي هذه التحذيرات في وقت تعاني فيه منطقة القرن الأفريقي، بما في ذلك الصومال، من آثار تغير المناخ، مما يزيد من حدة موجات الحر. في عام 2022، شهدت الصومال أسوأ موجة جفاف منذ عقود، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأزمات الصحية. مع تزايد الضغوط على النظام الصحي، يصبح من الضروري توعية الناس حول المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة، خاصة في ظل عدم الاستقرار السياسي والاقتصادي الذي تشهده البلاد.

articles.title: UNICEF demands truce, weapons ban to avert Sudan catastrophe
articles.summary: Deputy Executive Director Hannan Sulieman warns 80% of child casualties linked to drone strikes as fighting intensifies in North Kordofan
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. North Kordofan has seen a surge in drone strikes, with UNICEF highlighting that 80% of child casualties are linked to these attacks. The situation is exacerbated by the lack of adherence to previous ceasefire agreements, notably the one brokered in May 2023, as fighting continues to displace thousands.

articles.title: World Bank-Backed 3 Billion Birr Project to Revamp Power Distribution in Ethiopia
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) has officially signed a 3 billion birr grid rehabilitation and upgrade agreement with two international engineering and supply firms, Alpha TND and Capital Electech, to comprehensively modernize the power distribution networks in Western and Northwestern Ethiopia. This major infrastructure initiative specifically targets the upgrading of utility networks in three critical […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's power distribution has faced significant challenges due to ongoing conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, which has disrupted infrastructure and service delivery since the civil war began in November 2020. The Ethiopian Electric Utility's partnership with Alpha TND and Capital Electech, backed by World Bank funding, is crucial for restoring stability and improving energy access in the affected areas, aligning with the government's broader efforts to rebuild and foster economic recovery post-conflict.

articles.title: Taza Legal Moves to End Ethiopia’s Reliance on International Firms
articles.summary: Four Ethiopia’s prominent law offices have officially merged forces to create a major institution named Taza Legal LLP. This merger unites Tameru WondimAgegnehu & Partners, Mesfin Tafesse & Associates (MTA), Mekdes & Associates, and Hanna Mulugeta Law Office into a localized corporate powerhouse designed to challenge the traditional dominance of foreign law firms. Historically, complex […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's legal landscape has been historically dominated by foreign firms, particularly following the 2018 reforms that opened the economy to international investment. The merger of Taza Legal LLP comes at a time when the Ethiopian government is emphasizing national sovereignty and local capacity building, especially in light of ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and the need for legal expertise to navigate complex issues arising from the conflict and economic recovery efforts.

articles.title: Kenyan court bars media from covering deadly school fire trial
articles.summary: 8 girls face murder charges over dormitory blaze that killed 16 students
articles.context: The trial follows the tragic dormitory fire at Moi Girls High School in September 2023, which claimed the lives of 16 students and sparked national outrage over school safety regulations. The Kenyan government has faced criticism for inadequate fire safety measures in schools, prompting calls for reform. The case highlights ongoing tensions between the need for justice and the protection of minors in the legal system, particularly in high-profile cases involving youth.

articles.title: Abay Bank S.C. Debuts on ESX Main Market
articles.summary: Abay Bank S.C. has officially been listed on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX), becoming the fourth commercial bank to make its stock market debut. The Exchange welcomed the
articles.context: Abay Bank S.C.'s listing on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed at liberalizing the economy and attracting foreign investment. This move follows the signing of the 2020 Pretoria Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the country after the Tigray conflict, highlighting the government's efforts to foster economic recovery and growth in a post-conflict environment.

articles.title: Spiro closes $270 million round with new $55 million backing from NewTrails Capital
articles.summary: By our staff reporter African electric mobility platform Spiro has closed its latest funding round at $270 million after securing a fresh $55 million investment from Chinese growth-stage fund NewTrails Capital. The company said the new capital will support expansion of its battery-swapping network, manufacturing footprint and electric vehicle infrastructure across high-growth African markets. Spiro […]
articles.context: Spiro's funding comes at a crucial time as Ethiopia and neighboring countries are increasingly prioritizing sustainable energy solutions amid ongoing economic challenges and climate change impacts. The African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizes the need for infrastructure development, and the recent peace agreement in Ethiopia (November 2022) has opened avenues for investment in green technologies. This funding could enhance regional cooperation in electric mobility, aligning with Ethiopia's ambitions to become a leader in renewable energy.

articles.title: Beyond Discounts: Building a Sustainable Electronic Taxi Industry
articles.summary: #Advertorial The electronic taxi industry in Ethiopia has grown rapidly over the past decade, transforming the way people move around cities and creating new economic opportunities for thousands of drivers. As competition intensifies and new players enter the market, consumers are benefiting from lower fares, promotional offers, and a wider range of choices. While affordable […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's electronic taxi industry has emerged against a backdrop of rapid urbanization and economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018. The government's push for digitalization and foreign investment, coupled with the ongoing Tigray conflict and its impact on infrastructure, has created both challenges and opportunities for the sector. The recent influx of new ride-hailing apps, such as ZayRide and Ride, highlights the competitive landscape as the country seeks to stabilize and grow its economy.

articles.title: Oboth-Oboth Promises a New Era in Parliament, Says His Speakership Is a ‘Miracle’ That Will Silence Doubters
articles.summary: The Speaker of Parliament, Rt. Hon. Jacob Oboth-Oboth, has pledged to steer the legislature toward a more respected The post Oboth-Oboth Promises a New Era in Parliament, Says His Speakership Is a ‘Miracle’ That Will Silence Doubters appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: Jacob Oboth-Oboth's election as Speaker of Parliament comes amid South Sudan's fragile peace following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS). His leadership is seen as pivotal in uniting a fractured legislature, particularly after years of political turmoil and civil war that have undermined governance. The ongoing tensions between rival factions, including the SPLM-IO and SPLM, further heighten the stakes of his speakership.

articles.title: Over 1,400 displaced in Sudan's North Darfur in single day: UN
articles.summary: Fresh displacement from 5 villages in North Darfur comes days after another wave of forced movement as insecurity continues to escalate
articles.context: The recent displacement in North Darfur follows a surge in violence linked to ongoing clashes between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, which intensified after the military coup in October 2021. The 2020 Juba Peace Agreement aimed to address grievances but has largely failed to stabilize the region, leading to increased attacks on civilians and humanitarian crises, particularly in areas like El Fasher and Kutum.

articles.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
articles.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.
articles.context: The protests in Kenya stem from widespread discontent over the government's handling of economic issues and alleged electoral fraud during the 2022 presidential elections, which saw William Ruto assume power amid accusations of irregularities. The 2024 demonstrations, marked by violent clashes with security forces, resulted in the deaths of dozens, prompting ongoing calls for accountability from human rights organizations and opposition leaders like Raila Odinga, who continue to challenge the government's legitimacy.

articles.title: Kenyans mark two years since Gen Z protests
articles.summary: Two years on, families of those killed during Kenya’s 2024 anti-government protests returned to Parliament to remember their loved ones and demand justice.
articles.context: In 2024, Kenya experienced widespread anti-government protests triggered by allegations of electoral fraud and rising living costs, leading to violent clashes between demonstrators and security forces. The protests resulted in numerous fatalities, with families of victims still seeking accountability amid ongoing tensions between President William Ruto's administration and opposition groups, particularly the Azimio coalition led by Raila Odinga. The commemoration of the protests highlights the persistent struggle for justice and political reform in Kenya.

articles.title: Families honor victims as Kenya marks anniversary of deadly youth-led protests
articles.summary: Commemorations coincide with fresh protests, arrests in Nairobi
articles.context: The anniversary of the youth-led protests in Kenya, which erupted in 2020 following the police killing of George Floyd-inspired demonstrations, highlights ongoing tensions between the government and civil society. The protests have been fueled by widespread discontent over police brutality and economic hardships exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic. Recent arrests during commemorations in Nairobi reflect the government's continued crackdown on dissent, raising concerns about human rights and political freedoms ahead of the 2024 elections.

articles.title: طلاب سودانيون يعودون إلى معهد النور للمكفوفين برغم الدمار
articles.summary: تفاقمت معاناة المكفوفين في السودان منذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، بعدما تعرض المعهد الوحيد الذي يقدم لهم الخدمات التعليمية لدمار واسع.
articles.context: تسارعت وتيرة الصراع في السودان منذ أبريل 2023، عندما اندلعت اشتباكات عنيفة بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تدهور الأوضاع الإنسانية. معهد النور للمكفوفين، الذي يعد المعهد الوحيد في البلاد المخصص لتعليم المكفوفين، تعرض لدمار كبير، مما زاد من معاناة هذه الفئة المهمشة في ظل غياب الدعم الحكومي والموارد اللازمة لإعادة تأهيله.

articles.title: Authority Registers Bunna Bank Shares, Approves New Offering
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has approved the registration of more than 61.8 million shares held by shareholders of Bunna Bank S.C. and authorized the issuance of an additional 2.6 million shares to existing investors, the regulator said on Thursday. A public notice, ECMA said it had approved the bank’s registration statement, registering 61,895,598 […]
articles.context: The approval of Bunna Bank's share registration comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This move is significant as it reflects the government's efforts to stabilize the economy following years of conflict, particularly the Tigray War, which has strained financial resources and investor confidence. The recent peace agreement in November 2022 has also created a more favorable environment for investment.

articles.title: ECMA Approves Registration of Nearly 9.7 Million Abay Bank Shares
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has approved the registration of 9.66 million existing shares held by shareholders of Abay Bank, marking another step in the development of Ethiopia’s nascent capital market. Abay Bank said ECMA had approved the registration statement covering 9,657,286 existing shares owned by the bank’s shareholders. The approval became effective on […]
articles.context: The approval of Abay Bank's share registration comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing its economy and developing a capital market, a process initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This move is particularly significant as it follows the 2021 establishment of the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority, which is tasked with regulating the newly opened capital market, fostering investor confidence, and addressing the economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict.

articles.title: Demand Outstrips Supply in NBE’s USD 100 Million Forex Auction
articles.summary: – Banks Bid Above USD 160 Million The weighted average bid submitted by 14 commercial banks reached 157 Birr per U.S. dollar in the National Bank of Ethiopia’s latest foreign exchange auction held on Wednesday. The central bank offered USD 100 million for sale, attracting total bids worth USD 160.5 million. Nine commercial banks secured […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a severe foreign exchange shortage exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which began in November 2020. The government's efforts to stabilize the economy, including the recent signing of a peace agreement in November 2022, have not fully alleviated the pressure on the forex market. The National Bank of Ethiopia's auction reflects the urgent need for foreign currency amid rising inflation and economic instability.

articles.title: 215 civilians die in RSF-run prison in South Darfur: Sudanese medics
articles.summary: Sudan Doctors Network reports spread of diseases and epidemics inside Daqris Prison
articles.context: The deaths of 215 civilians in Daqris Prison highlight the dire humanitarian crisis in Sudan, exacerbated by the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) since April 2023. The power struggle following the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019 has led to widespread violence and instability, with the RSF's control over prisons raising concerns about human rights abuses and inadequate medical care for detainees.

articles.title: WHO warns about worsening humanitarian crisis, cholera outbreak in Sudan's Kordofan region
articles.summary: More than 700 suspected cases, 105 deaths reported in West Kordofan since mid-May, according to authorities
articles.context: The Kordofan region of Sudan has been plagued by conflict since the Darfur War began in 2003, with ongoing violence between government forces and various armed groups, including the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North. The political instability intensified following the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019, leading to a power vacuum and worsening humanitarian conditions. The recent cholera outbreak compounds the crisis, as access to healthcare and clean water remains severely limited amidst ongoing clashes.

articles.title: Central Bank’s FX Auction Sees Average rate Settles at 157 Birr per Dollar
articles.summary: The average rate of Ethiopian Birr (ETB) has slightly strengthened against the US dollar in the foreign exchange trading on Tuesday, the results released by the Central Bank
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges due to the ongoing Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, leading to disruptions in trade and foreign investment. The government's recent efforts to stabilize the economy, including reforms and engagement with international financial institutions, have heightened the importance of foreign exchange auctions. The Central Bank's latest auction results reflect these efforts amid a backdrop of rising inflation and a depreciating currency, impacting both businesses and consumers.

articles.title: Explainer: Critical constraints risk further Ebola spread as cases surpass 1,000
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – As the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with cases already surpassing 1,000 and
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak is exacerbated by the fragile health infrastructure in the DRC and Uganda, which have been historically affected by conflict and instability. The 2013-2020 Ebola outbreak in West Africa highlighted the region's vulnerabilities, while the recent resurgence has strained resources, particularly in border areas. Additionally, the lack of effective cross-border health agreements, such as the 2016 Kampala Declaration on Health Security, complicates coordinated responses to the outbreak.

articles.title: Human Rights Watch Rebukes Repressive Legislation in Tigray
articles.summary: Human Rights Watch has called on regional authorities in Tigray to immediately withdraw a piece of legislation that political observers have described as a “blueprint for authoritarianism.” The international watchdog issued the call after the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) introduced a sweeping security and mobilization proclamation earlier this month, forcing civilians into military service […]
articles.context: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has been at the center of a brutal conflict in Ethiopia since November 2020, when federal forces launched a military offensive against the TPLF, leading to widespread human rights abuses. The recent introduction of the security and mobilization proclamation in October 2023 has raised alarms, as it comes amid ongoing tensions following the November 2022 peace agreement, which aimed to end hostilities but has seen limited progress in restoring stability and governance in Tigray.

articles.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
articles.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.
articles.context: The tragic fire at Utumishi Girls' School on September 26, 2023, has reignited discussions about school safety and accountability in Kenya, particularly in the wake of ongoing debates about the management of educational institutions. The incident follows a series of school-related tragedies in the country, prompting the Ministry of Education to review safety regulations. The decision to charge the students with murder raises questions about juvenile justice and the broader implications for school governance in Kenya.

articles.title: Kenya signs $1.2B deal with Chinese firm to expand Nairobi airport
articles.summary: Project will nearly triple passenger capacity at East Africa's busiest aviation hub
articles.context: Kenya's decision to expand Nairobi's Jomo Kenyatta International Airport comes amid increasing competition in East Africa's aviation sector, particularly from Ethiopia's Addis Ababa Bole International Airport, which has been rapidly growing. The $1.2 billion deal with the Chinese firm China Road and Bridge Corporation is part of Kenya's Vision 2030 initiative, aimed at transforming the country into a regional hub for trade and tourism, especially following the economic setbacks caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: EXPLAINER — Sudan war disrupts supplies of gum arabic, a hidden ingredient in everyday products
articles.summary: As fighting persists, Sudan’s globally important gum arabic industry faces mounting risks from insecurity and illicit trade
articles.context: Sudan is the world's largest producer of gum arabic, a key ingredient used in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. The ongoing conflict, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has severely disrupted production and supply chains. The instability has led to increased smuggling and illegal trade, threatening not only the livelihoods of local farmers but also global markets reliant on this essential commodity.

articles.title: ECMA Approves Registration of Bunna Bank’s Securities
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has finalised the registration of more than 64 million existing and new shares of Bunna Bank S.C, a public notice the regulatory
articles.context: The approval by the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration. This move is significant as it reflects the government's efforts to stabilize the economy following the Tigray conflict, which has strained financial resources and investor confidence. The registration of Bunna Bank's shares is a crucial step toward enhancing transparency and attracting foreign investment in Ethiopia's banking sector.

articles.title: Ethiopia Selected as Africa’s Meteorology and Hydrology Training Center
articles.summary: It has been officially announced that the Ethiopian Meteorology Institute and Arba Minch University have been formally approved to serve as Regional Training Centers (RTC) for the World Meteorological Organization (WMO). This decision was passed during the 80th session of the World Meteorological Organization’s Executive Council meeting currently being held in Geneva, Switzerland. It was […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's selection as a Regional Training Center comes amid ongoing challenges, including the Tigray conflict that has strained the country's resources and international relations. The decision, made during the WMO's 80th Executive Council meeting in June 2023, highlights Ethiopia's commitment to enhancing regional climate resilience, particularly as the Horn of Africa grapples with severe droughts and climate change impacts. This approval also reflects a shift towards regional cooperation in addressing environmental challenges.

articles.title: Ethiopia Highlights Benefits of New Africa-Wide Biotechnology Information Portal
articles.summary: Ethiopia is set to benefit from improved access to biotechnology and biosafety information following the launch of a new continental digital platform aimed at enhancing transparency and supporting evidence-based decision-making across Africa. The newly introduced Biotech Africa Database, developed by the International Service for the Acquisition of Agri-biotech Applications (ISAAA AfriCenter) and its partners, brings […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's launch of the Biotech Africa Database comes amid ongoing challenges in agricultural productivity exacerbated by climate change and regional conflicts, particularly the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020. With Ethiopia's economy heavily reliant on agriculture, this initiative, supported by ISAAA AfriCenter, aims to enhance food security and promote sustainable agricultural practices, crucial for stabilizing the region and fostering cooperation among neighboring countries like Sudan.

articles.title: China’s new governance pitch aims at the world’s next frontiers
articles.summary: Beijing has a familiar message for a fragmented world: the global system is under strain, but it does not need to be torn down. It needs, China says, to be made more just, more equitable and better suited to the realities of a turbulent century. That is the central argument of a new white paper, […]
articles.context: China's renewed focus on governance comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a two-year civil conflict that ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to stabilize and rebuild, China's proposal for a more equitable global system resonates, particularly given its investments in African infrastructure through the Belt and Road Initiative. This context highlights China's role as a key player in shaping Ethiopia's political and economic landscape during a critical transitional period.

articles.title: Why Ethiopia Should Study China’s New White Paper
articles.summary: As Ethiopia’s new government takes shape, it should look carefully at China’s latest white paper on global governance — not to copy it wholesale, but to draw practical lessons from a development model that has transformed the country from poverty to power. China’s document, More Just and Equitable Global Governance: China’s Principles, Proposals and Actions, […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The new government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, faces challenges in rebuilding the nation and addressing economic instability. As Ethiopia seeks to enhance its global standing and foster development, examining China's governance model could provide valuable insights for its own political and economic strategies.

articles.title: Guterres Slams Fossil Fuel Windfall Profits, Urges Nations to Tax Extra Gains
articles.summary: The Secretary-General of the United Nations, António Guterres, has strongly condemned the “excessive windfall profits” amassed by globally operating fossil fuel companies. At a time when citizens with modest savings and the working class worldwide are facing skyrocketing energy bills, the Secretary-General stated that it is completely unjust and unacceptable for oil giants to collect […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of a two-year civil conflict that has exacerbated economic challenges, including rising energy costs. The Ethiopian government, facing pressure to stabilize the economy and address public discontent, is particularly sensitive to global energy price fluctuations. Guterres' call for taxing fossil fuel profits resonates in this context, as Ethiopia seeks to balance its energy needs while managing the socioeconomic fallout from the conflict and rising inflation.

articles.title: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
articles.summary: The scale and brutality of the sexual attacks is unprecedented and aimed at terrorising communities, the UN says.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated after the military coup in October 2021, which ousted the transitional government and reignited violence between rival factions, particularly the Sudan Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. The UN's recent findings highlight a disturbing trend of sexual violence being weaponized, particularly in regions like Darfur, where historical grievances and ethnic tensions have fueled brutal attacks since the Darfur conflict began in 2003.

articles.title: The Passing of a Shepherd of Ideas and a Servant of Humanity
articles.summary: With profound sorrow and deep gratitude, I mourn the passing of my dear friend and brother, Professor Fantu Cheru — an extraordinary Ethiopian and African intellectual whose life embodied the rare harmony between thought and action, scholarship and service, intellect and humility. Fantu’s journey — from the hills of Gondar to the lecture halls of […]
articles.context: Professor Fantu Cheru's passing comes at a time when Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. His contributions to peacebuilding and intellectual discourse are particularly poignant as the nation seeks to rebuild and reconcile amidst ongoing ethnic tensions and political instability. Cheru's work in advocating for social justice and development resonates deeply in this challenging context.

articles.title: Sexual violence used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan conflict: UN report
articles.summary: UN rights office verifies 546 cases across country, calls for independent investigations and accountability
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The UN report highlights the systematic use of sexual violence, exacerbating the plight of civilians amidst a backdrop of political instability following the ousting of former President Omar al-Bashir in 2019 and the subsequent power struggle. This situation underscores the urgent need for accountability and international intervention.

articles.title: Kenya minister halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
articles.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.
articles.context: The construction of the US-backed Ebola quarantine center in Kenya has sparked controversy amid ongoing tensions between the Kenyan government and local communities over health infrastructure. In 2021, the Kenyan High Court ruled against the facility, citing concerns over land use and community consent. Aden Duale's defiance of this ruling highlights the ongoing struggle between government authorities and judicial decisions, reflecting broader issues of governance and accountability in Kenya's political landscape.

articles.title: Kenya's health minister apologizes to court over controversial US-backed Ebola facility
articles.summary: Work on disputed health facility stopped, Aden Duale tells court
articles.context: The controversy surrounding the US-backed Ebola facility in Kenya stems from a 2014 agreement between the Kenyan government and the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, aimed at enhancing regional health security. However, local communities have raised concerns about land acquisition and potential health risks, leading to protests and legal challenges. The recent apology from Health Minister Aden Duale reflects the heightened tensions and the government's struggle to balance international partnerships with local sentiments.

articles.title: South Sudan’s Unfinished Transition: The Search for Common Ground
articles.summary: By: Francis Deng (PhD) & Amir Idris (PhD) South Sudan’s transition remains unfinished, not only because of weak institutions, repeated political crises, and a lack of political will, but also because the country has yet to agree on common ground. At the heart of the problem is a deeper question: what kind of state, system […]
articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but has been mired in civil conflict since 2013, primarily between factions loyal to President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish a power-sharing government, yet implementation has faltered, leaving the country grappling with ongoing violence, economic instability, and a lack of consensus on governance structures.

articles.title: As Aid Shrinks, Development Leaders Search for a New Model
articles.summary: As foreign aid budgets contract across much of the developed world and debt pressures mount in poorer countries, development leaders meeting in Nairobi this week are debating whether the decades-old aid model can survive a rapidly changing global economy. The debate is unfolding at a two-day conference hosted by the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom, […]
articles.context: The ongoing economic challenges in Kenya, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising global inflation, have led to significant cuts in foreign aid, which traditionally supported development projects. Additionally, the recent droughts and conflicts in neighboring regions, such as Ethiopia's Tigray conflict, have strained resources further. The Nairobi conference comes at a critical time as Kenya seeks to redefine its development strategy amidst these pressures, particularly following the 2021 Nairobi Declaration on Sustainable Development.

articles.title: South Sudan sets December date for long-delayed first-ever election
articles.summary: Plans could yet be scuppered again over logistical issues and continued tensions and violence.
articles.context: South Sudan's first-ever elections, scheduled for December 2023, follow years of civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to a fragile peace agreement in 2018. The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) aimed to unify rival factions, but ongoing violence and disputes over security arrangements threaten the electoral process. Key actors include President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar, whose rivalry continues to fuel instability in the region.

articles.title: Ethiopian Welcomes First Twin Otter Classic 300-G Aircraft from De Havilland Canada
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopian Airlines has taken a delivery of its First Twin Otter Classic 300-G aircraft from De Havilland Canada today, June 22, 2026. The delivery comes
articles.context: The delivery of the Twin Otter Classic 300-G aircraft comes at a crucial time for Ethiopian Airlines, as the country is recovering from a two-year civil conflict in the Tigray region that ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. This acquisition signals a push towards revitalizing the aviation sector and enhancing connectivity in a region still grappling with economic challenges and the need for humanitarian access.

articles.title: شعبة مستوردي الوقود: عجز تمويلي بقيمة 326 مليون دولار يفاقم أزمة النقد الأجنبي في السودان
articles.summary: أكدت الغرفة القومية لمستوردي المواد البترولية أن أزمة النقد الأجنبي والارتفاع الأخير في أسعار الوقود خلال النصف الأول من عام 2026 يرتبطان بعوامل اقتصادية عالمية ومحلية، نافية مسؤولية شركات الاستيراد عن تراجع قيمة العملة الوطنية. وقالت الغرفة، خلال مؤتمر صحفي عقدته بمدينة بورتسودان، إن التوترات في مضيق هرمز تسببت في ارتفاع حاد بأسعار المشتقات النفطية … The post شعبة مستوردي الوقود: عجز تمويلي بقيمة 326 مليون دولار يفاقم أزمة النقد الأجنبي في السودان appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تواجه السودان أزمة اقتصادية خانقة منذ انفصال جنوب السودان في 2011، حيث فقدت البلاد حوالي 75% من إنتاجها النفطي. تفاقمت هذه الأزمة بسبب النزاعات المستمرة في دارفور والنيل الأزرق، بالإضافة إلى تأثير العقوبات الاقتصادية الدولية. في عام 2023، أدت التوترات في مضيق هرمز إلى ارتفاع أسعار الوقود، مما زاد من الضغوط على الحكومة الانتقالية في السودان التي تسعى لإصلاح الاقتصاد المتعثر.

articles.title: لجنة المعلمين السودانيين تعلن بداية تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم
articles.summary: قالت لجنة المعلمين السودانيين إن تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم بدأ اليوم وفق الجدول المعلن من لجنة الإضراب، مشيرةً إلى استمراره حتى يوم غدٍ الثلاثاء. وأفادت اللجنة في منشور على “فيسبوك”، أنّ اللجنة العليا للإضراب ستعقد اجتماعها يوم الجمعة المقبل لتقييم الخطوة الحالية ودراسة المستجدات واتخاذ ما تراه مناسباً من قرارات وخطوات قادمة. articles.title: Why Sustained Support for Refugees in Gambella Matters More Than Ever?
articles.summary: By Charles Ossey At just one month old, NyapilikaJany was fighting for her life in Jewi Refugee Camp in Ethiopia’s Gambella Region. Born weighing only 2.1 kilograms, the South Sudanese refugee infant was diagnosed with severe acute malnutrition and a respiratory infection. Her mother, Nyabel Deng, who fled conflict and sought refuge in Ethiopia in […]
articles.context: The Gambella Region in Ethiopia has been a refuge for South Sudanese fleeing the ongoing civil war that erupted in December 2013, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. As of 2023, over 400,000 South Sudanese refugees reside in Gambella, with the Ethiopian government and UNHCR working to provide aid amidst increasing tensions over resource allocation and security concerns. The recent escalation in conflict in South Sudan further exacerbates the urgent need for sustained humanitarian support.

articles.title: South Sudan sets Dec. 22 for long-awaited general elections

articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but has faced ongoing civil conflict since 2013, leading to a humanitarian crisis and political instability. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace and a transitional government, paving the way for these elections. The December 22 vote is crucial for consolidating peace and restoring public trust in governance after years of turmoil and leadership disputes.
